beats.text: A system label appears: d-u over d-t equals A times u. Two arrows emerge from the origin — each an eigenvector, pointing in its own pure direction.
Eigenvalue zero: the arrow along that eigenvector stays perfectly frozen. No growth, no decay — a steady state locked in place.
Eigenvalue negative three: the arrow along the second eigenvector shrinks toward the origin, collapsing exponentially. The solution melts away.
Flip that sign to positive three. The same arrow now rockets outward, stretching away from the origin without bound.
Now a complex eigenvalue: the solution arrow spirals — rotating and shrinking simultaneously, tracing a tightening curve toward the origin.
Every solution is a mix of these pure behaviors. Eigenvalues are the rates. Eigenvectors are the directions. Together they solve the whole system.
